params.name: Antarctic Treaty
params.description:
On Dec 1, 1959 AD, countries around the world signed the Antarctic Treaty.
Antarctica, the world's latest discovered continent and the only continent without permanent residents, is a land claimed by a number of countries. In order to regulate the activities of all countries, the Antarctic Treaty was signed. The treaty states that Antarctica is only for peaceful purposes, promotes scientific expeditions and international cooperation, military activity is prohibited in Antarctica, and freezes …
